prompt: Using ProteinMPNN with co-evolution constraints from a 2,000-sequence HBsAg MSA spanning all HBV genotypes (A–J), design novel sHBsAg surface-loop sequences with predicted enhanced cross-genotype immunogenicity for improved recombinant vaccine antigen production in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. outline a computational approach covering: (1) HBsAg MSA construction from NCBI HBV sequences and EVcouplings co-variation analysis, (2) ProteinMPNN design of the 'a' determinant loop (aa 100–160) with co-variation constraints, (3) immune epitope overlay with IEDB-annotated B-cell epitopes to preserve immunodominant sites, (4) yeast expression test prediction and in silico immunogenicity scoring. Discuss regulatory implications for next-generation HBV vaccine antigens.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and NCBI HBV sequence database; target: HBsAg large envelope protein (L-HBsAg, P03140 as canonical reference); organism: Hepatitis B virus; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: viral envelope protein (small HBsAg, major vaccine antigen)
